meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve can't finish projects (Advanced Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome can't finish projects. A Advanced-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: User Onboarding to Solve Project Stall
content.seo_description: Master user onboarding to fix project stalls. A high-level guide for students using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship your first professional SaaS app.
content.answer_block: Most student projects fail because they lack a defined user path. By implementing a focused onboarding flow, you force clarity on your core value proposition. This structural constraint limits feature creep, keeps your development scope tight, and ensures you ship a functional, user-ready product instead of an endless, unreleased prototype.
content.intro:
You have spent weeks in the dark, wrestling with complex state management and database schemas, only to watch your project gather digital dust in a private GitHub repository. The excitement of the 'idea phase' has long since evaporated, replaced by the crushing reality of feature creep and technical debt. You are stuck in the infinite loop of the unpolished build.

But the secret to shipping isn't working harder—it is building for the user from day one. By prioritizing user onboarding, you transform your project from a sprawling experiment into a guided experience. This shift provides the guardrails necessary to finish, forcing you to define exactly what your app does and why it matters, ensuring you cross the finish line with a portfolio-ready asset.

steps.title: Define the 'Aha!' Moment
steps.description: Identify the single feature that provides immediate value. Document this as your onboarding destination.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project codebase and identify the core 'Aha!' moment. Create a user flow map that leads a new user to this feature in under 3 clicks.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'map user flow to core value proposition'

steps.title: Build a Frictionless Auth Wrapper
steps.description: Implement a minimal authentication flow that doesn't block the user from seeing the product's value.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold an authentication component using Clerk or Supabase that prioritizes guest access before requiring sign-up.

steps.title: Create the Interactive Walkthrough
steps.description: Develop a lightweight step-by-step UI tour that highlights the core UI elements.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React-based guided tour component using Shepherd.js to highlight the dashboard and primary action button.

steps.title: Implement Onboarding Analytics
steps.description: Track where users drop off during onboarding to identify friction points in your logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Integrate PostHog tracking events for each step of the onboarding flow to monitor conversion rates.

steps.title: Final Polish and Launch
steps.description: Clean up the UI states and deploy the onboarding flow to production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit the onboarding flow for mobile responsiveness and accessibility compliance (WCAG 2.1).

pro_tips: Never force sign-up before showing value; let users test your core utility first.
Use progressive profiling to collect user data only when absolutely necessary for the next step.
Keep your onboarding flow under 30 seconds to maintain high conversion rates for your MVP.
Design your onboarding as a state machine; it makes debugging logic errors significantly easier.
Acknowledge the user's progress with a visual progress bar to trigger the 'Zeigarnik Effect', encouraging completion.
content.next_steps: Once the onboarding flow is live, start sharing your project in public forums and track the analytics to see where users actually drop off. Iterate based on data, not assumptions.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting your projects stall at 80% completion. Master the art of onboarding and start shipping software that users actually understand and value. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
